behavior_summary: BACKPACK::look displays the item label "backpack " to the viewer when the player examines the backpack (Event 0). It prepares per-process context then calls the item's bark routine and suspends.

BACKPACK::use toggles the backpack UI: if the item's status word has bit 0x0100 set it opens the backpack gump with parameter 0x02; if that bit is clear it closes the gump and runs a cleanup routine to remove certain contained items.

BACKPACK::00AC scans the backpack's contents with nested iterators, tests contained-item status bits and shape/family values, and frees/destroys matching items via FREE::2767. This is invoked on gump-close to clean up special contained objects.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BACKPACK::BACKPACK::look: linear flow — set per-process info, call Item::bark with literal "backpack ", then suspend; no persistent state changes observed (confidence: high).

U8P_BACKPACK::BACKPACK::use: conditional branch based on Item::getStatus() & 0x0100. True -> Item::openGump(param=0x02). False -> Item::closeGump and call U8P_BACKPACK::BACKPACK::00AC for cleanup (confidence: high).

U8P_BACKPACK::BACKPACK::00AC: nested iteration over container contents; calls Item::getStatus for candidates, compares status bits and a shape/family constant (inferred 0x008F), and calls FREE::2767 to remove matches (confidence: medium).
open_questions: What is the exact semantic meaning of the status bit 0x0100 (appears to act as an 'open' or 'active' flag for the backpack gump, but this is inferred).

Which concrete item family/shape corresponds to constant 0x008F used in the cleanup comparison (the analysis infers an item type such as 'obsidian coin' in related scripts but this is not proven here).
Are there edge cases where BACKPACK::00AC removes items that should persist (timing/ownership rules) — the precise cleanup policy and why those shapes/statuses are chosen is unclear.
What process/pid is used when Item::bark is invoked from BACKPACK::look (pid is pushed implicitly; the exact caller-target mapping is inferred).

main_function.name: U8P_BACKPACK::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the 'U8P_BACKPACK::look' (examine) action for a backpack item: prepare per-process context and ask the item to 'bark' the label "backpack ", then suspend.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — triggered when the player/actor examines or looks at the backpack (area/inspect trigger).

function_map.name: U8P_BACKPACK::look
function_map.role: Display the item's label/description on examine (calls Item::bark).

function_map.name: U8P_BACKPACK::use
function_map.role: Handle use action: check item status bit 0x0100, open or close the backpack UI accordingly and run cleanup on close.

function_map.name: U8P_BACKPACK::00AC
function_map.role: Iterate backpack contents and free/destroy contained items that match specific status/shape criteria (cleanup routine invoked on close).

internal_calls.purpose: Invoke cleanup routine to scan and free certain contained items when the backpack is closed.

external_calls.purpose: Display the static string "backpack " to the viewing process/player; used to label the item on examine.
Read the backpack's status word to decide whether to open or close the gump (test bit 0x0100).
Open the backpack UI with parameter 0x02 when status bit 0x0100 is set (interpreted as 'open' guard).
Close the backpack UI when the tested status bit is clear before performing cleanup.
Query status bits on contained items to decide which ones are candidates for further inspection or removal.
Free/destroy contained items that match the inferred cleanup criteria (shape/family and status tests).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: BACKPACK::look displays the item label "backpack " to the viewer when the player examines the backpack (Event 0). It prepares per-process context then calls the item's bark routine and suspends.
